market_context.note: All-time highs on May 29; AI-fueled rally; Computex week ahead

tickers.thesis: Computex week catalyst (NVDA keynote expected); $6.5B photonics bet signals long-term data-center moat; first NVDA-powered Windows PC launching with MSFT. Short-term pullback (-1.45%) on profit-taking is buyable dip.

Strong single-day beat (+5.45%) driven by NVDA-powered Windows PC partnership announcement and Copilot momentum. Computex-adjacent beneficiary. Near-term follow-through likely but size down after the gap.
AWS AI infrastructure spending continues; Q1 2026 earnings beat. Mild -1.23% pullback within broader AI bull market. Long-term secular AI tailwind intact.
MI300X gaining data center traction as NVDA alternative; Computex-adjacent AI PC exposure. Slight underperformance vs market not concerning at -0.38%.
risks: Market share gains slower than projected

tickers.thesis: Declining after six-day rally; near-term mean-reversion risk. AI monetization story intact but valuation stretched post-run. Neutral stance; watch for reset entry.
WWDC approaching (typically early June) as next catalyst. Modest -0.37% pullback from ATH. No near-term earnings or product catalyst clear; defensive hold.
risks: AI integration pace slower than peers

tickers.thesis: Delivery growth slowing; FSD progress narrative vs execution gap. -1.43% mild underperformance. High beta, political risk via Musk-government ties. Abstain from new positions.
risks: Brand damage from Musk political exposure

tickers.thesis: Significant underperformance (-2.51%) vs market on up day — possible sector rotation from GOOGL to MSFT/NVDA. AI competition narrative (OpenAI, Anthropic, Perplexity) weighing. Abstain until structure clarifies.
risks: Search market share erosion from AI chatbots
GOOGL-specific rotation out of AI laggard

fom_notes: norm_sizing_sigma is sizing_sigma / max(sizing_sigma) across watchlist. recent_hit_rate seeded at 0.50 (uniform prior, day-1 no history). All inputs normalized to [0,1].
